title: Module 1
content:
Number system and codes:
Basics of Digital Electronics; Binary, octal, hexadecimal number system -
Representations, base conversions and arithmetic; Signed representation
of binary numbers-fixed and floating point ; 1’s and 2’s complement of
binary numbers and their arithmetic.
BCD number system- conversion of BCD to binary and Binary to BCD;
Gray codes; Excess 3 codes;
Basics of Verilog-basic language elements: identifiers, data objects,
scalar data types, operators

title: Module 2
content:
Boolean Algebra and Logic gates
Laws
of
Boolean
algebra-postulates-Simplification
of
Boolean
expressions; De-Morgan’s law- minimization of Boolean expressions;
Sum of product (SOP); Product of Sum (POS), Canonical form;
Karnaugh Map Simplification (upto 4 variables); Quine MucCluskey
method
Basic and universal gates- Universality of NAND and NOR, ; design of
simple logic circuits using logic gates
TTL and CMOS logic families- comparison, Internal Diagram of
TTL NAND gate and CMOS NOR gate

title: Module 3
content:
Combinational circuits:
Difference between combinational and sequential circuits; Half
adder/subtractor, Full adder/subtractor; Multiplexer (using logic gates)-
4:1,8:116:1;
De-Multiplexer
(using
logic
gates)--
1:4,1:8,1:16;
implementation of functions using
Multiplexer IC74151; Encoder
(4:2,8:3); Decoder (2:4,3:8), BCD Adder;
Modelling and simulation of combinational circuits with Verilog codes
at gate level (Adder, Subtractor, MUX,De-MUX)

title: Module 4
content:
Sequential circuits designs:
Introduction to latch, Flip Flops, FSM; concept of Edge triggering and
level triggering in FF; SR FF, JK FF, MS-JK FF, D FF, T FF-Truth table,
Excitation table and characteristics equation;
Counter designs-Asynchronous counter; Synchronous Counter; MOD
counters ; Ring Counter , Johnson Counter,
Shift registers using FFs- SISO,SIPO,PIPO,PISO;
Programmable Logic Devices- PAL, PLA,FPGA (Basic concepts only)
Modelling and simulation of JK and D flip flops in Verilog
